title: Device Request – Tuya Smart Socket with Energy Monitoring – _TZ3210_5ct6e7ye / TS011F
excerpt: ### Prerequisites: Before requesting a device addition, please ensure there is not already a request for the device among the open issues. Make sure your Homey is upgraded to firmware v5 or higher. You need a physical example of the device. ### Device Information Device Name: Tuya Smart Socket with Energy Monitoring Device Model: TS011F Device Description: 16A Zigbee smart socket with real-time energy monitoring (voltage, current, active power, accumulated energy). Includes USB‑PD 20W output (not independently controllable). Works as a Zigbee router. Link to device image: ### Device Interview ```json { "ids": { "modelId": "TS011F", "manufacturerName": "_TZ3210_5ct6e7ye" }, "endpoints": { "ieeeAddress": "[REDACTED_IPV6]", "networkAddress": 40534, "modelId": "TS011F", "manufacturerName": "_TZ3210_5ct6e7ye", "endpointDescriptors": [ { "status": "SUCCESS", "nwkAddrOfInterest": 40534, "_reserved": 30, "endpointId": 1, "applicationProfileId": 260, "applicationDeviceId": 266, "applicationDeviceVersion": 0, "_reserved1": 1, "inputClusters": [ 3, 4, 5, 6, 1794, 2820, 57344, 57345, 0 ], "outputClusters": [ 25, 10 ] }, { "status": "SUCCESS", "nwkAddrOfInterest": 40534, "_reserved": 10, "endpointId": 242, "applicationProfileId": 41440, "applicationDeviceId": 97, "applicationDeviceVersion": 0, "_reserved1": 0, "inputClusters": [], "outputClusters": [ 33 ] } ], "deviceType": "router", "receiveWhenIdle": true } } ``` ### Additional Comments This TS011F variant exposes full energy measurement clusters (metering and electricalMeasurement) and reports correctly at Zigbee level, but Homey does not map these capabilities. USB‑PD port is not an independent endpoint (expected behavior). Other TS011F devices are already supported; this one only requires adding the fingerprint for _TZ3210_5ct6e7ye. Device purchased from a Tuya OEM vendor. Two photos attached above for reference (front + internal Zigbee module). ### How to interview a device Add the device as a generic Zigbee device in Homey Navigate to https://developer.athom.com/tools/zigbee Interview the device (button to the right of the device in the list) Click the copy button/icon to capture the device information Paste the copied information above Note: To be able to add more devices to the Tuya Zigbee app, we rely on community members like you to provide interviews of the devices you want to be added. Thank you for your contribution!
aiAnalysis:
Based on the GitHub issue, here is the extracted structured device information:

**Device Name:** Tuya Smart Socket with Energy Monitoring

**Fingerprint:**
- **manufacturerName:** `_TZ3210_5ct6e7ye`
- **productId (modelId):** `TS011F`

**Device Type:** Smart Plug / Socket with Energy Monitoring (Zigbee Router)

**Key Clusters (Endpoint 1):**
- **0x0006 (OnOff):** Switch control
- **0x0702 (Metering):** Energy metering (accumulated energy)
- **0x0B04 (ElectricalMeasurement):** Voltage, current, active power
- **0xE000 / 0xE001 (Tuya):** Tuya proprietary clusters (57344, 57345)

**Capabilities Expected (based on clusters):**
- `onoff` (switch)
- `measure_power` (active power)
- `measure_voltage` (voltage)
- `measure_current` (current)
- `meter_power` (accumulated energy)

**Additional Notes:**
- USB-PD 20W output is present but not independently controllable (no separate endpoint).
- Device works as a Zigbee router (`deviceType: "router"`).
- The issue requests adding the fingerprint `_TZ3210_5ct6e7ye` to an existing TS011F driver (likely `plug_energy_monitor` or similar).
- No Tuya DP (0xEF00) cluster is present; this is a ZCL-native device using standard metering clusters.
